# 1000 GRE Words

| No. | Word | Definition | No. | Word | Definition |
| :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- | :-- |
| 264. | diurnal | Occurring every day; happening in the daytime (rather than at night) | 274. | droll | Funny in an odd way |
| 265. | diverge | Differ, deviate; branch off or turn aside, as from a path | 275. | dubious | Doubtful, questionable, suspect |
| 266. | divest | Deprive or strip of a rank, title, etc., or of clothing or gear; to sell off holdings (opposite of invest) | 276. | dupe | Person who is easily fooled or used (noun); to fool or exploit (verb) |
| 267. | divine | Discover through divination or supernatural means; perceive by insight | 277. | duplicity | Deceit, double-dealing, acting in two different ways to deceive |
| 268. | doctrinaire | Person who applies doctrine in an impractical or rigid and close-minded way (noun); merely theoretical or fanatical about others accepting one's ideas (adj) | 278. | dyspeptic | Grumpy, pessimistic, irritable; suffering from dyspepsia (indigestion) |
| 269. | document | Support with evidence, cite sources in a detailed way, create documentary evidence of | 279. | ebullient | Very enthusiastic, lively, excited; bubbling as though being boiled |
| 270. | doff | Take off (such as clothes), put aside; remove one's hat as a gesture | 280. | eccentric | Peculiar, odd, deviating from the norm, esp. in a whimsical way |
| 271. | dogma | A system of principles laid down by an authority; established belief | 281. | eclectic | Selecting the best of everything or from many diverse sources |
| 272. | dormant | Asleep, inactive, on a break | 282. | edify | Uplift, enlighten, instruct or improve in a spiritual or moral way |
| 273. | dovetail | Join or fit together | 283. | efficacy | The quality of being able to produce the intended effect |
